decision: First-page 304 → empty delta for that endpoint
why: issues/releases fetched newest-first (sort=created&direction=desc), so any new in-window item changes page-1 ETag; a 304 provably means nothing new
alternatives_rejected: conditional on all pages (unnecessary, later pages never hold newer items)
If-Modified-Since instead of ETag (weaker validator, deferred)

decision: New ConditionalCache port + JsonFileETagStore adapter, injected into the collector; persist to a SEPARATE etags.json
why: preserves collector pure-I/O boundary (AC-2-015) — it depends on a port, not the State Store; separate file avoids bumping state.json locked version:1 and keeps best-effort vs fatal corruption semantics distinct
alternatives_rejected: store ETags inside state.json (bumps locked schema; conflates best-effort optimization with fatal-on-corrupt idempotency store)
collector reads/writes etags.json directly (breaks pure-I/O boundary + injectability)

decision: set() in-memory; pipeline commit() only AFTER mark_seen; abort-before-commit leaves etags.json unchanged
why: RISK-001 lost-item bug — committing an ETag for items fetched-but-not-yet-recorded would make the next run 304-skip un-rendered items (silent data loss, violates idempotent/never-lose-item non-negotiable)
alternatives_rejected: collector commits immediately on 200 (crash between 200 and mark_seen loses items)
per-repo commit inside the loop (a later fatal repo still loses the just-committed repo's un-persisted seen-state on abnormal exit)

decision: Conditional requests gated by etag_cache_enabled AND delta_enabled
why: delta_enabled=false means 'render every item every run' (V1 behavior); a 304-driven empty delta would wrongly suppress items, so ETag caching must be off when delta is off
alternatives_rejected: gate on etag_cache_enabled only (breaks delta_enabled=false contract)

decision: etags.json is best-effort: corrupt/missing → empty cache + WARN, never fatal
why: it is a pure rate-limit optimization; losing it only forces unconditional fetch, never changes collected items — deliberate opposite of state.json StateError-on-corrupt
alternatives_rejected: mirror state.json fatal-on-corrupt (would make an optimization file able to abort the whole run — wrong risk tradeoff)

decision: Injected ConditionalCache defaults to a no-op null cache; GitHubClient Protocol unchanged
why: mirrors Redis SummaryCache injection (_build_cache/_NullCache); a collector built without a cache behaves exactly as today (additive on cache miss); adapter-only ctor arg keeps the frozen Protocol intact
alternatives_rejected: add a method to GitHubClient Protocol (Protocol is frozen; would ripple to all implementers)

decision: [ASSUMED] a 200 with no ETag header → record nothing / clear stale, no crash
why: proxies/edge cases may omit ETag; dirty-data tolerance — next run just goes unconditional
source: analyst inference from AC-2-010 dirty-data tolerance; validate at SPEC-LOCK

decision: [ASSUMED] v2-002 single-instance lock already serializes runs, so the ETag store needs atomic-write torn-read safety but no own cross-process lock
why: lock.py fcntl.flock serializes runs against the state dir

decision: Discussions (GraphQL) never conditional; fetch_discussions unchanged
why: GitHub GraphQL API does not support ETag/If-None-Match — REST-only feature per kickoff
alternatives_rejected: attempt a GraphQL-level cache (no ETag support; out of scope)

decision: JsonFileETagStore is best-effort corrupt-tolerant — INVERT json_store.load(): missing/empty/corrupt/unreadable → empty dict + WARN, never raise
reasoning: etags.json is a pure rate-limit optimization; losing it costs one unconditional refetch, never correctness — deliberate opposite of state.json fatal-on-corrupt (AC-V2-007-004)
rejected: reuse json_store raise-on-corrupt (would let an optimization file abort the run)

decision: set() mutates in-memory dict; commit() is the sole atomic disk write; _NullConditionalCache no-op is the default ctor arg + disabled/failed-build result
reasoning: enables the crash-safe commit-after-mark_seen ordering (no per-item I/O) and removes null checks at call sites — mirrors _NullCache for Redis (AC-V2-007-005/007/008)
rejected: set() writes through to disk (breaks RISK-001; N disk writes)
optional cache|None + if-cache checks scattered across collector+pipeline

decision: Conditional on newest-first FIRST PAGE only; _classify(304)→_Action.OK and fetch branches on raw status_code==304 → return [] ; pages 2..N unconditional
reasoning: issues sort=created&direction=desc / releases newest-first → any new in-window item changes page-1 ETag, so a page-1 304 provably means nothing new (RISK-002); reuses _request_with_retry verbatim (AC-V2-007-010/011/013/016)
rejected: conditional on every page (unsound; later pages hold no newer items)
new _classify NOT_MODIFIED action (over-engineered; OK+raw-status branch suffices)

decision: run_pipeline calls conditional_cache.commit() exactly once after _collect_all returns, UNGUARDED (no try/except, not per-repo, not earlier)
reasoning: RISK-001 crash-safety — a fatal AuthError/StateError propagates before the commit line so etags.json stays unchanged and the next run re-fetches; terminal RateLimitError is caught inside _collect_all (break+partial) so commit runs for completed repos whose items were mark_seen-recorded (AC-V2-007-024/025)
rejected: per-repo mid-loop commit (muddies the unchanged-on-abort guarantee for the whole run)

decision: Collector calls set() only when the 200 response carries an ETag header; a stale entry is left in place (no active clear)
reasoning: resolves S1 [ASSUMED] AC-V2-007-014 — a present-guard satisfies 'record nothing, no crash'; a stale ETag is harmless (next run just gets a fresh 200); actively deleting adds code for no correctness gain; set(key,"") rejected (malformed If-None-Match)
rejected: set(key,"") on missing ETag (sends empty/malformed header)
actively delete the stale key (no correctness benefit)

decision: _NullConditionalCache moved to ports.py (not etag_store.py as task 1.3 specified)
reasoning: Existing test_collector_is_pure_io_no_state_or_llm asserts osspulse.cache never imported in client.py (AC-2-015). Placing null object in ports.py is architecturally correct — it is a port-layer concern. etag_store.py re-exports it. No functional change.
rejected: Keep in etag_store.py + suppress test (breaks isolation contract)

decision: extra_headers passed as empty dict {} not None to httpx.get()
reasoning: or {} guard handles both None and empty dict uniformly; httpx merges empty dict cleanly with client headers
rejected: Pass None directly (httpx accepts None but the guard is cleaner)

decision: _ensure_loaded() lazy-loads once on first get/set call; commit() short-circuits if cache is None
reasoning: Matches design ADR-002; prevents unnecessary file I/O if store is never accessed; clean sentinel
rejected: Eager load in __init__ (breaks best-effort pattern for cold construction)

decision: 0 bugs found — full code review of 5 risky areas (commit placement, 304 branch, best-effort load, GraphQL isolation, two-flag gate) and 3 test files; all 28 ACs pass independent verification
reasoning: RISK-001 tripwire: test_commit_not_called_on_auth_error_mid_loop and test_commit_called_exactly_once_after_collect_loop both pass; commit() unguarded after _collect_all; ADR-004 contract intact

decision: GraphQL path confirmed untouched — fetch_discussions: no extra_headers, no cache.get/set; test_fetch_discussions_sends_no_conditional_header + test_fetch_discussions_cache_set_never_called both pass
reasoning: AC-V2-007-017 / BR-V2-007-012 enforced; GraphQL POST unchanged from v2-006

decision: Best-effort semantics confirmed — _ensure_loaded() never raises on corrupt/missing/unreadable; all 3 WARN+empty paths tested; zero StateError import in etag_store.py
reasoning: ADR-001 inversion of json_store.py fatal semantics is correctly implemented; test_corrupt_json_returns_empty_and_warns passes

decision: Security: token sentinel not in etags.json or conditional-path logs/errors; test_token_not_in_conditional_path_error_message + test_token_sentinel_never_in_etags_json pass
reasoning: RISK-003 reaffirmed; etag_store only stores what set(key, validator) receives; error messages log type(exc).__name__ not exc string

decision: GO — 0 bugs Critical/High/Medium/Low; 28/28 ACs independently verified; 609/609 tests pass; 97% coverage on touched modules; RISK-001 tripwire pass; ruff clean
reasoning: All acceptance criteria met; no spec/design gaps; no hollow TCs; DEVIATION-001 architecturally correct and accepted
